Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

STT 1, 070 [Hymn to Ninurta][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[a-na] iš-di-iḫ ne₂-ber ⸢ka⸣-a-ri ša₂ ⸢šu-uḫ-mu⸣-ṭa-at a-lak-⸢tu₂ LUGAL kiš⸣-[ša₂-ti ...]

[For] profitable business at the crossing of the port where the journey is quickest, O [...] king of the world, [...].

o 22

[i]-na qe₂-reb te-še-e a-šar ša₂-⸢ta-at⸣ a-nun-⸢tu be⸣-el DINGIR-⸢MEŠ pa-ši⸣-lu [...]

In the middle of chaos, where the fight is joined, O lord of the gods, [...] the crawling one [...].

o 33

mu-tu ša₂ kak-ki šit-pu-ru-ma uz-za lab-šu qar-rad qar-⸢ra⸣-di tu-⸢šat-bak⸣ a-⸢kam₂⸣-[ma ...]

A warrior who is fitted out with weapons (and) clothed with wrath, O hero of heroes, you make the weak one cast (him) aside [and ...].

o 44

ḫaš-ḫa-ša₂ a-ku-u ša₂ la-mu-šu qab-lu a-ša₂-red ⸢a-ša₂-re-du⸣ ina ⸢bi⸣-ri-šu-nu iṣ-ṣu-[riš ...]

The lame (and) the weak, whom battle surrounds, O most pre-eminent one, [...] from between them [...] like a bird.

o 55

mun-nar-bu pe-tan bir-ki ša₂ la a-ni-ḫa bir-ka-a-šu mu-⸢šim⸣ NAM-MEŠ ina da-um-⸢ma⸣-[ti ...]

The fleet-footed, the swift runner, whose knees are tireless, O decreer of destinies, [...] in the darkness.

o 66

ša ina u₂-ṣu it-teb-bu-⸢u₂-ma?⸣ ša₂ ki-ma ṣu-⸢ṣe⸣-e la-mu-u₂ ab-ra-[a-ti]

He who attacks with arrows, whom people surround like a reed-thicket,

o 77

nu-ru nam-ru e-li pa-ši-⸢li⸣ an-dul-la-ka ⸢tu⸣-[šat-ra-aṣ]

O bright light, you make your protection stretch out over the crawling one.

o 88

ša₂ la i-šu-u₂ mam-ma-na a-šib ⸢IRI⸣-ka muq-⸢qu e⸣-[tel] DINGIR-⸢MEŠ šar⸣-ḫu la mu-da-a ⸢mam-ma⸣-an!(MAN) tu-⸢šar⸣-[šaš]

He who has nobody, a weakened inhabitant of your city, O proud lord of the gods, you arrange for him to get somebody he does not know.

o 99

⸢ša₂⸣ da-du-šu šar-ru um-ma la i-ṣab-ba-tu₂ ŠU-su ḫa-a-a-⸢iṭ kul-lat⸣ gim-ri ina ŠA₃ ek-le-ti ⸢tu⸣-[kal-lam-šu₂ ...]

He whose favourite is a young child whose mother does not grasp his hand, O examiner of absolutely everything, you show him [...] in the darkness.

o 1010

[ša₂] ⸢i⸣-na ID₂ <<⸢u₃⸣>> gišMA₂.GUR₈ u₃ ina ur-ḫi ⸢gišmar?⸣-tur-u₂ la u₂-⸢qa⸣-[a-šu₂]

He for [whom] on the river no boat and on the path no small vehicle waits,

o 1111

[ḫa-a-a]-⸢iṭ⸣ AN.⸢TA⸣-MEŠ ba-ru-u KI.TA-MEŠ ina qe₂-reb ti-amat gal-la-ti GIM a-[gi-i ...]

O examiner of the upper world, scrutiniser of the lower world, [...] inside the rolling sea like a wave.

o 1212

[...] ⸢en-šu⸣-u-ti mu-saḫ-ḫi-ru ⸢a⸣-ku-u₂-ti be-lu₄ dNIN.URTA!(UR) ša₂ ina na-as₂-pan-ti e-⸢zi⸣-[bu ...]

[...] of the weak, who gives attention to the cripple, O lord Ninurta, who leaves [...] in devastation.

o 1313

[iṣ-ṣur le]-⸢e⸣-ḫi ša₂ ka-mu-u iṣ-⸢ṣu⸣-ri ik-su-u i-di-šu be-lu₄ ta-ḫa-na-at DINGIR-MEŠ [...]

The lēhu-[bird], whose wings the bird catcher bound, O lord, help of the gods, [...].

o 1414

[... pa-su]-⸢un-tu⸣ [ik-tu-mu]-⸢ši⸣-ma na-ra-⸢tu?⸣ [...]

[...] which [he covered] with a net and which is struck [...],

o 1515

[... ina] ⸢pi⸣-iq-ti i-ni [...]

[... from] the fine mesh [...].

(rest of obverse missing)
Reverse
rr NaN  (start of reverse missing)
r 1'r 1'

[... dun-na-ma]-⸢a e-la?⸣ [...]

[...] a person of lowly status without [...],

r 2'2'

[...] x [...] ⸢u₃⸣ ana-ku a-⸢ku⸣-[u ...]

[...] ... [...] and I, the weak one, [...].

r 3'3'

[... ša₂ i]-na i-ni nu-mer-ku-u i-[na ...]

[... who] is late in the eyes, [...] in [...]

r 4'4'

[...] x ba-al-ti ta-nam-din-šum-ma e-⸢rak?⸣-[...]

[...] ... of dignity, you give him ... [...].

r 5'5'

[... mu-du]-⸢u₂⸣ ša₂ ki-ma ša₂-a-ri a-na me-ḫi-il-ti [...]

wise [...] who like the wind for a blow [...],

r 6'6'

[... ṭup]-šar-ru-tu ki-ma gigu-ru-um-me-e ⸢kar⸣-[šu-uš-šu ...]

[...] the scribal art like a reed gurummû-object [...] in his mind,

r 7'7'

[be]-⸢el nab-ni⸣-tu₂ ši-kin₃ da-nu₃ DUB.SAR ba-an-de-e ⸢el⸣-[šu₂? ...]

O lord of creation, appearance of Anu, [...] a bandû-scribe over [him],

r 8'8'

⸢U₄⸣ BI? ana MA.NA-a-<a?> uṣ-ṣab ur-qa u₃ šu-a-te ana pi-i-šu ul ub-ba-al u₄-mu-šu₂ [...]

that day he will add ... for each mina and he will not bring that to his mouth. On that day [...]

r 9'9'

na-ša₂-a re-e-ši šar-ra-ḫu-u₂ e-la-a ka-a-ta ti-ʾu-us-si-⸢na⸣ [...]

O honoured one, very proud one, apart from you, [...] their sustenance?

r 10'10'

muš-ta-mu-u₂ mun-dal-ku a-ḫi-iz rid-di man-nu ša₂-nin-ka i-na DINGIR-MEŠ ki-⸢ma⸣ [...]

O considerate one, counsellor, well-behaved one, who is your rival among the gods? [...] like [...].

r 11'11'

lu₂ENGAR i-ke-e-šu A-MEŠ ⸢šaḫ⸣-lu-⸢uq⸣-ti it-ba-lu-ma uš-ta-ni-<<TAB>>-⸢ḫu⸣ [...]

The farmer whose dykes the waters of destruction have carried off so that he became depressed [...],

r 12'12'

le-ʾu-u it-pe-šu be-el me₂-reš-ti kit-te ta-paq-qid-su-ma ana [...]

O powerful one, expert, lord of true cultivation, you entrust him to [...]

r 13'13'

id-da-kaš-šum-ma ša₂ i-ṣa u₂-ḫal-li-qu i-rab-šu [...]

will be raised for him and the one who lost a few, he will requite him [...].

r 14'14'

⸢IRI?⸣-ka NIBRUki ša₂ ug-ga-tuk in-ne₂-⸢ep⸣-[šu]

Your city Nippur which was built in your rage,

r 15'15'

[...] ze-ra-ni-ma la ⸢i⸣-ḫal-li-qa eš-[re-tu₂?]

[...] the descendants so that the chapels are not lost.

r 16'16'

[ṣi-ru]-ut-ka lu!(LA) e-mu-qa-a-a-⸢ma⸣ da-lil-ka ina [...]

May your supremacy be my strength and [...] your praise in [...].

partial ruling
(colophon)
r 17'17'

[... m]⸢d⸣30—DU₃ lu₂ŠAB.TUR TUR DUMU mdAMAR.UTU—DU₃—DUMU.UŠ lu₂A.BA lu₂ḪAL [...]

[...] of Sin-ibni, young scribal apprentice, son of Marduk-ban-apli, scribe, diviner [...].
